volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 2.2.235b - Section 2.4.285)

sections.label: Section 2.2.235b
sections.translation: To your house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.235c
sections.translation: And I to your house , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 2.2.235d
sections.translation: Why thither ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.235e
sections.translation: What ’ s that to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.236
sections.original: Enim non ibis nunc , vicissim nisi scio .
sections.translation: Why , you shan ’ t go now , unless , in return , I know .

sections.label: Section 2.2.236b
sections.translation: You are teazing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.236c
sections.translation: I choose to .

sections.label: Section 2.2.237
sections.original: numquam hercle istuc exterebrabis , tu ut sis peior quam ego siem .
sections.translation: Never , upon my faith , shall you wring this out of me , so as to prove yourself more artful than I am .

sections.label: Section 2.2.238
sections.translation: It ’ s a misery to contend with you in artfulness .

sections.label: Section 2.2.238b
sections.translation: You are a mischievous baggage .

sections.label: Section 2.2.239
sections.translation: What is there for you to fear ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.239b
sections.translation: The very same that there is for you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.239c
sections.translation: Say then , what is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.240
sections.original: ne hoc cuiquam homini edicerem , omnes muti ut loquerentur prius .
sections.translation: But I ’ m forbidden to tell this to any person , and am instructed that all the dumb people are to speak of it before myself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.241
sections.original: Edictum est magno opere mihi , ne quoiquam hoc homini crederem ,
sections.translation: And most especially was I cautioned not to trust this to any person , so that all the dumb people were to mention this before myself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.242b
sections.translation: Still , do you do so ; on giving our words , let ’ s trust each other .

sections.label: Section 2.2.243b
sections.translation: I know this — all procuresses are light of faith , and the weight of a water-gnat [*] is not more light than is the word of a procurer .

sections.label: Section 2.2.245
sections.translation: Tell me , there ’ s a dear .

sections.label: Section 2.2.245b
sections.translation: Tell me , there ’ s a dear .

sections.label: Section 2.2.245c
sections.translation: I don ’ t want to be your dear .

sections.label: Section 2.2.245d
sections.translation: You ’ ll easily prevail upon me in that .

sections.label: Section 2.2.246
sections.translation: Keep it yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.246b
sections.translation: And you be mum about this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.246c
sections.translation: It shall be kept a secret .

sections.label: Section 2.2.246d
sections.translation: It shall not be known .

sections.label: Section 2.2.247
sections.original: Toxilo has fero tabellas tuo ero .
sections.translation: I ’ m carrying this letter to Toxilus , your master .

sections.label: Section 2.2.247b
sections.translation: Be off ; he ’ s there at home . And I am carrying this pinewood tablet sealed , to Lemniselene , your mistress .

sections.label: Section 2.2.249
sections.translation: What ’ s written there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.249b
sections.original: Iuxta tecum , si tu nescis , nescio ;
sections.translation: If you don ’ t know , pretty much like yourself , I don ’ t know ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.250
sections.translation: except soft words , perhaps .

sections.label: Section 2.2.250b
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.250c
sections.translation: And I ’ ll be off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.250d
sections.translation: Move on then .

sections.label: Section 2.3.251
sections.translation: To Jove the opulent , the renowned , the son of Ops [*] , the strong , the mighty in power , who riches , hopes , kind plenty does bestow , joyously and gratefully do I offer the sacrifice of thanksgiving ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.255
sections.original: quiá meo amico amíciter hanc cómmoditatis cópiam
sections.translation: inasmuch as in a friendly way they have bestowed for my friend this opportunity of satisfying his necessity and of borrowing the money , so that I can lend him aid in his need . Whereas I no more dreamed , or thought , or imagined that I should have this opportunity — that same has now fallen from heaven as it were .

sections.label: Section 2.3.259
sections.original: nam erus meus me Eretriam misit , domitos boves ut sibi mercarer ,
sections.translation: For my master has sent me to Eretria [*] ; he has given me the money to purchase some trained oxen for him ; but he said that the fair would take place seven days hence ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.261
sections.original: stultus , qui hoc mihi daret argentum , cuius ingenium noverat .
sections.translation: a simpleton to trust this money to me whose propensity he knew ; for this money I shall misapply in some other purpose : there were no oxen for me to buy . Now I ’ ll both promote the success of my friend , and will give my inclination full enjoyment . The pleasures that belong to a long time will I serve up in a single day . Crack , crack [*] it will be upon my back ; I don ’ t care .

sections.label: Section 2.3.265
sections.original: nunc amico homini boves dómitos mea ex crumina largiar .
sections.translation: Now , to a person that is my friend I shall present these trained oxen from out of my purse ; for this , in fact , is a delightful thing , handsomely to bite your thrice-dotted niggardly , antiquated , covetous , spiritless people , who against their servant seal up the saltcellar with the salt . It ’ s a virtue , when occasion prompts , to hold them in contempt . What will he do to me ? He ’ ll order me to be beaten with stripes , the fetters to be put on . I may get a beating .

sections.label: Section 2.3.270
sections.original: ne sibi me credat supplicem fore : vae illi , nil iam mihi novi
sections.translation: Don ’ t let him fancy that I shall go begging to him . Woe be unto him ! Nothing new can now be inflicted upon me but what I have already experienced it . But see , here comes Paegnium , Toxilus ’ s boy .

sections.label: Section 2.4.272
sections.original: Pensúm meum , quod datumst , cónfeci . nunc dómum propero .
sections.translation: My task that was set me I ’ ve finished ; now I ’ m hastening home .

sections.label: Section 2.4.272b
sections.translation: Stop , although you are in haste — Paegnium , listen to me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.273b
sections.original: Emere oportet , quem tibi oboediré velis .
sections.translation: You ought to buy a person , for you to desire to be obedient to you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.273c
sections.translation: Stop there , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.4.274
sections.original: Exhibeas molestiam , ut opínor , si quid debeam ,
sections.translation: You ’ d be giving some trouble , I fancy , if I were to be owing you anything ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.275
sections.original: qui nunc sic tamen es molestus .
sections.translation: who are now so troublesome .

sections.label: Section 2.4.275b
sections.translation: You rascal , will you look back then ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.276
sections.original: Scio ego quid sim aetatis , eo istuc maledictum impune auferes .
sections.translation: I am aware of what age I am ; for that reason you shall get off for this abuse with impunity .

sections.label: Section 2.4.277
sections.translation: Where is Toxilus , your master ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.277b
sections.original: Vbi illí libet , néque te consulit .
sections.translation: Wherever he pleases , and he don ’ t ask your advice .

sections.label: Section 2.4.277c
sections.translation: Won ’ t you tell me , then , where he is , you villain ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.279
sections.translation: I don ’ t know , I say , you elm-twig spoiler [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.280
sections.translation: You are abusive to your senior .

sections.label: Section 2.4.280b
sections.translation: As you deserved it first , do you put up with it . My master bade me hold my labour at his bidding , my tongue in freedom .

sections.label: Section 2.4.281
sections.translation: Will you not tell me , where is Toxilus ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.281b
sections.translation: I tell you that — you may go to perdition everlasting .

sections.label: Section 2.4.282
sections.translation: This day you shall be flogged with a rope ’ s end .

sections.label: Section 2.4.282b
sections.translation: On your account , indeed , you cuckoo ! I ’ faith , you carrion , if I were to give you a broken head , I shouldn ’ t be afraid of that .

sections.label: Section 2.4.284
sections.original: Video ego te : iam incubitatus es .
sections.translation: I understand you , you ’ ve been up to [*] some bad work just now .

sections.label: Section 2.4.284b
sections.original: Ita sum . quid id ad te attinet ?
sections.translation: So I have . What business is that to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.285
sections.original: at non sum , ita ut tu , gratiis .
sections.translation: But I haven ’ t , like yourself , all for nothing .
